steps.title: Compare the means
steps.content:
Let $S$ be the sum of the $57$ integers in data set F. Since each value in F is at least $160$,
$$S\ge57\cdot160=9{,}120$$Adding $12$ to make data set G must lower the mean, not raise it.

steps.title: Compare the medians
steps.content:
Let the sorted values in F be listed in order. The median of F is $a_{29}$. In G, $12$ becomes the first value, so the median is
$$\frac{a_{28}+a_{29}}{2}\le a_{29}$$Thus the median of F is not less than the median of G.

steps.title: Evaluate both statements
steps.content: Neither the mean nor the median must be less for data set F than for data set G. The answer is D.
